name: 03 — Email Intake (dev@ → ticket → Claude Code)
description: Watches dev@ inbox. Parses email with Claude. Creates ticket in Supabase + GitHub issue. Triggers Claude Code Web with structured prompt.

modules_overview.purpose: Watch dev intake inbox (TBD — alias not yet provisioned for namkhan-bi; defer until set)
modules_overview.config_notes: Connect Gmail. Filter: only emails from PBS's own address (sender allowlist).
Route 1: text only. Route 2: voice attachment (.m4a, .mp3). Route 3: image attachment.
OpenAI Whisper API. Append transcription to email body.
Model: claude-opus-4-7. System prompt: see PARSE_INTENT_PROMPT below. Input: email subject + body. Output: JSON {arm, intent, summary, clarifying_questions: []}.
Route A: clarifying_questions.length > 0 → reply with questions. Route B: arm='research' → trigger Research Arm. Route C: arm='dev' → trigger Dev Arm.
Insert with status='clarifying' or 'in_progress' depending on route
POST https://api.github.com/repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues with parsed summary as title + body

modules_overview.purpose: Trigger Claude Code Web (for dev tickets)
modules_overview.config_notes: POST to Claude Code Web webhook (TBD — confirm endpoint with Anthropic docs) with prompt referencing the GitHub issue and CLAUDE.md context

modules_overview.purpose: Auto-reply to PBS with ticket # and status
modules_overview.config_notes: Reply-all to original email. Subject: Re: [original]. Body: ticket #, what's being done, ETA.

manual_setup_steps: 1. Set up a dev intake alias forwarding to PBS Gmail (alias address TBD for namkhan-bi)
2. Create Make.com scenario, add Gmail trigger
3. Add sender allowlist filter (only PBS's address)
6. Add Supabase insert + GitHub issue creation
7. Add Claude Code Web trigger (when API confirmed)
9. Test by emailing dev@: 'Test ticket — change footer color to red' (don't actually run)
10. Verify ticket created, auto-reply received, no actual code changes
PARSE_INTENT_PROMPT:
You are an intent parser for a hotel/hospitality dev pipeline. Read the email and return JSON only.

Fields:
- arm: one of 'dev' | 'research' | 'fix' | 'decide' (NOT clear → set 'dev' if user explicitly says 'build/add/change', else 'research')
- intent: 'build' | 'investigate' | 'fix' | 'decide'
- summary: 1-2 sentence rephrase of the request
- target: which project/site (Namkhan / unknown) — namkhan-bi repo only
- clarifying_questions: array of strings — only include if confidence <80% on what they want. Max 3 questions.
- estimated_complexity: 'small' | 'medium' | 'large' (small = <2h, medium = 2-8h, large = full day+)
- requires_approval: true | false (true if touches auth/payment/booking-write/schema)

Rules:
- If email mentions 'check / why / what's wrong / how to / better / compare' → arm='research'
- If email mentions 'build / add / change / create' → arm='dev'
- If email mentions 'broken / error / not working' → arm='fix'
- If email asks 'should we / which' → arm='decide'
- If user provides a URL or specific page reference, include in summary
- Output ONLY valid JSON, no markdown, no preamble
